person.name: Seilbek Skindirov
person.title: Software Developer · Full-Stack Engineer
person.tagline: 17+ years across the stack — from GPS, GSM and VoIP gateways early on to CRM/ERP integrations and payments more recently.

about: Full-stack and backend engineer with 17+ years building backends, integrations and full-stack products.
The work has ranged from web platforms with payment and banking integrations and, recently, CRM/ERP synchronization, to GPS/GSM/VoIP gateways and access-control device software earlier in my career.
Comfortable across the stack — backend services, integration entry points, data-synchronization pipelines, and migrating monoliths to microservices and Docker.

experience.summary: Tools for automation and synchronization of business workflows.
highlights: Designed and implemented unified integration entry points for CRM/ERP systems including Repfabric, Salesforce, Oasis, Fishbowl, AutoQuotes and QuickBooks.
Maintained and enhanced MS Outlook and Gmail add-ons/extensions to meet business requirements.
Built data-synchronization pipelines for bidirectional dataset transfer between third-party systems.
Expanded unit and integration test coverage across multiple services.
Maintained and adapted a legacy system to evolving business requirements.
Contributed to the architecture and design of new backend services.
Proposed and implemented CI/CD pipelines using TeamCity.

experience.summary: National test-preparation platform for high-school graduates in Kazakhstan.
highlights: Led frontend and backend development, including a full migration from desktop to web application.
Integrated payment systems and banking APIs into the platform.
Migrated the platform to microservices and Dockerized the production infrastructure.
Designed and maintained internal and external task-management workflows.
Built a web portal for medical staff to manage patient-related workflows.
Created several internal tools to address specific business requirements.

experience.summary: Access-control hardware integration (alongside a full-time role).
highlights: Established communication between turnstile access-control devices and a backend server.
Consulted on GPS device integration during development of an internal tracking service.
Developed tools to automate internal workflows.
Maintained and extended a legacy dispatch-management system.
Created accounting tools to address business reporting requirements.
Implemented GPS integration, including custom server/client development and in-vehicle device setup.
Designed and implemented a GSM gateway using an internal modem via AT commands.
Designed and implemented a VoIP gateway based on Asterisk and the SIP protocol.

experience.summary: Government department of the Kostanay region (alongside a full-time role).
highlights: Implemented a real-time internal chat application.

projects.name: Beacon
projects.summary: Production Telegram bot and Mini App tracking FX and precious-metal rates across national banks, built as a multi-binary Go service.
highlights: Multi-binary architecture — separate collector, notifier, web, migrator and operator (doctor) processes over a shared pure-Go SQLite store.
WASM Telegram Mini App for per-user subscriptions: sparkline charts, rate history, and delta / interval / daily / cron alert conditions.
Versioned REST API with an embedded operator dashboard; LLM-assisted extraction-rule generation in the doctor tool.

projects.name: YardDog
projects.summary: Self-hosted home-network watchdog that monitors the internet uplink and reboots a GPON router when the link goes down.
highlights: Cron-fired, port-less watchdog on a Raspberry Pi — detects outages against independent IP and DNS targets, then reboots the router by driving its web UI (session login, per-request CSRF token).
Records every check, reboot and recovery phase in SQLite, announces reboots over Telegram, and exposes the history as JSON through an optional read-only LAN query daemon.
Pure-Go, cgo-free host telemetry (CPU, memory, disk, temperature, network counters); cross-compiles for linux/arm64, amd64 and Apple Silicon.

projects.name: VPNTunnel
projects.summary: Forward HTTP proxy routing all egress through a bearer-authenticated WireGuard tunnel — no root, no kernel modules.
highlights: Userspace networking via gVisor netstack; HTTP forward and HTTPS CONNECT in one static binary.
Constant-time token authentication.

projects.name: NeuroCompass
projects.summary: Commissioned Telegram Mini App for cognitive self-analysis, delivered to production.
highlights: Full Go backend: SQLite storage and OpenRouter LLM integration.
Seven-screen UI.

projects.name: LogInjector
projects.summary: Concurrent-safe structured logging library: fan-out to multiple sinks with level-targeted hooks.
highlights: Pluggable handlers — console, rotating file, Telegram — composed as io.Writer sinks.
HTTP middleware preserving Flusher/Hijacker/Pusher; auto-redacts authorization headers.

projects.name: DSNInjector
projects.summary: Go library for parsing and building Data Source Names — connection strings, environment variables and data-source configuration.
highlights: Parses a DSN into structured accessors: driver, host, port, database and query options.
No runtime dependencies beyond the standard library.

projects.name: WhoAmI
projects.summary: A static-site generator in Go that builds a personal CV / portfolio into plain static HTML at build time — no backend to run.
